differential_diagnoses: Intracranial hypotension (due to recent paracentesis/dural leak)
Intracranial hemorrhage or underlying mass lesion
next_steps: Immediate ophthalmology consultation to rule out arteritic anterior ischemic optic neuropathy
Measure erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) and C-reactive protein (CRP) levels
Perform magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain and magnetic resonance venography (MRV)
Perform lumbar puncture for opening pressure and cerebrospinal fluid analysis
Temporally withhold terlipressin to assess for possible drug-induced intracranial pressure changes
Start high-dose intravenous glucocorticoids empirically if suspicion for giant-cell arteritis remains high
Perform immediate ophthalmological examination including fundoscopy to check for papilledema
Obtain MRI and MRV of the brain to evaluate for vascular, structural, and parenchymal abnormalities
Measure erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) and C-reactive protein (CRP) levels
Temporarily discontinue terlipressin while evaluating for drug-induced vascular effects
Review coagulation profile including INR and platelet count prior to any invasive procedures
Initiate high-dose intravenous glucocorticoids if suspicion for giant-cell arteritis remains high, given the risk of permanent visual loss
